summary: Today's AI news highlights China's growing presence in the AI industry, with companies like Moonshot and Alibaba unveiling models that rival those of OpenAI and Anthropic. Meanwhile, US public health agencies are testing AI models, and Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI may disrupt its IPO plans.
insight: The increasing competition in the AI industry is driving innovation, but also raises concerns about bias, job displacement, and the need for responsible AI development.
highlights: China's Moonshot AI unveils Kimi K3 open-weight model, rivaling OpenAI and Anthropic
US public health agencies test OpenAI and Anthropic AI models
Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI may disrupt its IPO plans

articles.title: AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring

articles.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
articles.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.

articles.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
articles.description: Current AI, a non-profit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat and more.

articles.title: Will AI fix prior authorization—or make it worse?
articles.description: The government is piloting a program that uses AI for insurance-coverage decisions.

articles.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
articles.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

articles.title: A scorecard for the AI age
articles.description: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.
